title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that enables scalable data processing and analysis. It consists of the following components:

- Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS): stores the input data and intermediate results.
- MapReduce framework: schedules and executes the tasks on the cluster.
- Map tasks: process the input data and generate intermediate key-value pairs.
- Reduce tasks: process the intermediate data and generate the final output.
- YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator): manages the resources and schedules the tasks on the cluster.

The MapReduce architecture follows the divide and conquer pattern, where the input data is divided into smaller chunks and processed in parallel. The intermediate results are stored in HDFS and processed by the Reduce tasks. The YARN manages the resources and schedules the tasks on the cluster to optimize the performance and efficiency.

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that enables scalable data processing and analysis. It consists of the following components:

- Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS): stores the input data and intermediate results.
- MapReduce framework: schedules and executes the tasks on the cluster.
- Map tasks: process the input data and generate intermediate key-value pairs.
- Reduce tasks: process the intermediate data and generate the final output.
- YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator): manages the resources and schedules the tasks on the cluster.

The MapReduce architecture follows the divide and conquer pattern, where the input data is divided into smaller chunks and processed in parallel. The intermediate results are stored in HDFS and processed by the Reduce tasks. The YARN manages the resources and schedules the tasks on the cluster to optimize the performance and efficiency.
